name: OpenSSF Scorecard Defines Dependency Exposure Review Signals
statement: The OpenSSF Scorecard check documentation defines dependency exposure signals across dependency update tooling, pinned build and release dependencies, SBOM availability, and known open vulnerabilities checked through OSV-backed data, while also noting that automated detection does not prove operational execution or project-specific risk.
time_horizon: OpenSSF Scorecard check documentation retrieved on 2026-06-01.
quantitative_note: No quantitative estimate; this is a technical documentation extraction.
caveats: This finding describes Scorecard check semantics and does not evaluate a specific repository.
A detected tool, SBOM, lockfile, or vulnerability signal still needs repository-specific context before making assurance or exploitability claims.
The source itself documents that some checks have detection limits and that low scores are not always definitive proof of project risk.

domain_fields.control_signal: Update automation, pinning, SBOM, and known vulnerability evidence signals
domain_fields.risk_interpretation: Supports a bounded baseline claim that dependency exposure review should separate inventory, vulnerability, and update-posture evidence from final risk interpretation
domain_fields.limitations: Does not verify dependency completeness, exploitability, or remediation status for any concrete project
